title: Caution when removing and installing the fuel tube with quick connectors

blocks.text: Caution when removing and installing the fuel tube with quick connectors
Quick connectors (A), (B), and (C) are used at the following connection points in the engine compartment and fuel tank

items.text: Between fuel feed tube (D) and fuel pipe (E)
Between fuel feed tube (D) and fuel feed pipe (F)
Between fuel pump (G) and fuel feed tube (H)

blocks.text: Since the quick connector type fuel tube must be removed when removing and installing the fuel pipe, fuel pump, and fuel tank, the following items must be observed when removing and installing the fuel pipe, fuel pump, and fuel tank.

items: Do not use a rag with battery acid on it. If it adheres to the fuel tube, replace the fuel tube.
Do not forcibly bend or twist the fuel tube when attaching or detaching the connector. If it is bent, be sure to replace it with a new one.

blocks.text: If the connector is disconnected when the fuel tank is removed or installed, it is designed to be reconnected. However, if the retainer remaining on the pipe side is removed from the pipe, the retainer cannot be reused. Therefore, in the following cases, be sure to replace the retainer with a new one.

items.text: To replace the fuel feed pipe
When the retainer is removed from the pipe
Scratches, damage, etc. to the retainer

items: Retainers vary in shape depending on the manufacturer and size, so be sure to use retainers of the same make and size according to the table. Use of a retainer of a different make and size may cause fuel leakage and must be strictly observed.
When installing a new one, install a retainer of the same color.
